enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a life update after nearly three months, covering his marijuana dispensary experience in Boulder, extensive Google Earth data collection work (Roman roads, tribes, rivers, battle locations) and repeated data losses, a project pairing Boulder/Front Range geography with Roman history to help people calibrate historical sizes and distances, plans for audio book/bike ride pairings, his hamster's death, and his general daily routine of working on website projects.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Dense, heavy weed is better value than the poofy stuff that burns away quickly
Boulder's marijuana laws are weird and constantly changing, especially the cash-only and no-bank-account rules
Cycling and audiobooks are a perfect pairing — like the hand was made for the glove
Better to ask 'where will I be in a year' than set a hard deadline, because progress itself is rewarding enough
Working hard daily, expects to be ready to launch bike/history content around summer 2016

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did a video vlog about living in Boulder because it's been almost three months.

claims.subject: Randy
claims.claim: Randy wrote me on Facebook and suggested I do a video vlog about living in Boulder.

claims.subject: Chris
claims.claim: Love for nature wrote me a couple days ago saying that Chris travels and that a video shot him out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm really high right now because I smoked weed for like a half hour to get extra high for the video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I accidentally left my weed in the living room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to a new dispensary today because I usually go to The Farm, but today's Sunday and I couldn't get my before 10 deal (15% off if you go there before 10).

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had trouble finding the new dispensary because I was high and must have been really high, even though I didn't smoke weed this morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was sober this morning, which is rare for me, and maybe that's why I was super confused.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I typed the wrong address into my phone and ended up going to the wrong place.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dispensary was right where the lady said it would be, but I didn't see it at first.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent $100 on weed at the new dispensary, but it was way less weed than I usually get—like a quarter or something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last time I went to The Farm, I paid $91 out the door for a half ounce.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The weed I got at the new dispensary is super dense like hash, so a little bit burns for a long time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The Farm sells poofy weed that burns away quickly, which I think is crappy stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder County law doesn't allow dispensaries to do the stamp card thing anymore (10 times you buy, they give you a stamp).

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Dispensaries have to film you all the time and show your ID when you first go in and again when you actually get the weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Dispensaries have to buy everything in cash, except the place I went to today, which takes credit cards but treats it like cash.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The Farm used to take credit cards but can't now because of the new law.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Dispensaries can't open bank accounts because of federal law, so they have to store all their cash in cash.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The guy at the new dispensary was super high, with totally red eyes, and was really relaxed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The weed I got has a really sweet smell, like the best weed from 20 years ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was so high at the start of this video that I had low blood sugar and felt a little dizzy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot what I was going to talk about in this video because I was so high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been collecting Google Earth data for the last three months, tracing roads, tribes, rivers, and trails.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lost two weeks' worth of Google Earth data because I transferred it to the wrong folder and most of it got lost.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lost the Arizona Trail, all the rivers, tribes, and other data I had collected.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started collecting battles from Wikipedia, tracing their exact locations.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I re-did all the tribes and rivers in Europe, as well as linguistic groups in Italy and Greece.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm tracing motor bike routes, mountain bike routes, road bike routes, running routes, and kayaking routes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to make a Google Earth download page on my website for all this data.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on a project to show passing speeds of people in different modes of movement (biking, running, kayaking, etc.) from 33 mph down to 2 mph in quarter-mile increments.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating a fantasy world around where I live to help people understand the size of ancient kingdoms by comparing it to Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The ancient kingdom of Rome was about 20 kilometers in diameter, which is the size of the current ring road of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm using Haystack Mountain as the center of my fantasy world because it's 300 feet higher than Neverro and about the size of the Great Pyramid of Egypt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm pairing the history of Boulder with the history of Rome, matching battles, conquests, and land acquisitions to the same sizes and distances.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm tracing summits and creating routes for biking, running, and climbing, with different colors for different terrains.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm pairing bike rides with audiobooks, so the ride takes as long as the audiobook.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm selling my van to pay off debts and plan to build a new panel with help from a mechanic.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My hamster died a couple days ago, maybe because I didn't clean the cage for a month.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a couple of geckos, and they're doing well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to audiobooks at double speed, which is about the speed I'm talking now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I run through audiobooks one after another, which is the best way to do it.
